name: early-warning-score
title: Early Warning Score
description: An aggregate deterioration score (NEWS2, MEWS, PEWS) with its parameter breakdown and its trajectory. Displays a score — never computes one.

props.name: system
props.description: Required. "NEWS2", "MEWS", "PEWS" — the instruments are not interchangeable, so the chart names the one in use.

props.name: score / band
props.description: The aggregate score and its band, both from the consumer's validated logic.

props.name: parameters
props.description: `{ label, value, points }` — the contributions, so a score can be checked rather than trusted.

props.name: history
props.description: The score over time. The trajectory is the reading.

props.name: escalationThreshold
props.description: The caller's protocol threshold, drawn as a labelled line.

props.name: guidance
props.description: The caller's escalation guidance. Local protocol, not the component's.

clinicalNotes: The component displays a score; it does not compute one. NEWS2, MEWS, and PEWS are validated instruments with defined scoring tables, and a UI library that reimplemented one would be shipping an unvalidated clinical calculator that no consumer could audit or version. The parameter breakdown exists so a clinician can check the arithmetic against the chart rather than trust a number. The trajectory is given as much room as the value, because a score of 4 that was 1 four hours ago is a different patient from a score of 4 that has been 4 all day, and a rising score still inside the "low" band is exactly the signal an aggregate tends to flatten — hence the explicit "Rising — was N" badge. Escalation guidance is the caller's local protocol; the component never asserts what to do.
notes: Feeds naturally into ai-escalation-alert. Composes risk-score-gauge + chart + badge.
